sections.title: 1. PROSE: Earth, the Desolated Home
sections.content:
Story Overview

  • 🚀 Setting: The year 2068. Earth is completely destroyed due to human exploitation.
  • 🛰️ New Habitat: Humans have colonized Mars (the Red Planet). India has established three colonies there.
  • 👨‍👩‍👦 Characters: Arivumathi and her grandfather.
  • 📖 Plot Synopsis: Grandfather travels 1500 sols (Martian days) to visit Arivumathi for her birthday. He compares the harsh, artificial life on Mars with the beautiful, natural past of Earth.
  • 🌱 Core Message: We must protect our natural home (Earth) instead of destroying it and struggling to adapt to dead planets.

Key Comparisons (Earth vs. Mars)

Feature Life on Earth (Past) Life on Mars (Year 2068)
Environment Clean, natural, and free air. Toxic; requires a space suit and oxygen cylinder.
Agriculture Natural, fertile soil. Vegetables were tasty. Soil is chemically treated. Crops lack real flavor.
Water Supply Naturally available from rain and rivers. Artificially generated by burning fuels.

sections.title: 2. POEM: Beyond the Universe
sections.content:
Theme: A vivid, imaginative journey across the solar system, exploring the unique characteristics of different planets.

The Cosmic Journey:

  • 🌍 1. Earth: Leaving behind our blue home.
  • 🔴 2. Mars: Gliding past the red planet.
  • 🪐 3. Saturn: Marveling at its magnificent rings and moons.
  • 🌀 4. Jupiter: Observing the solar system's giant, characterized by its red storms.
  • 🔃 5. Uranus: Passing the tumbling, rolling planet.
  • ❄️ 6. Neptune: Flying past the blue ice giant.
  • 🌌 7. Final Destination: Reaching beyond the Milky Way galaxy, soaring into the infinite universe to meet the Creator.

sections.title: 3. SUPPLEMENTARY: Lost in Space
sections.content:
Story Overview

  • Main Characters: Two friends, Amuthan and Nilavan.
  • The Incident: While visiting a space museum, they accidentally lock themselves inside a functional space shuttle. They accidentally pull a lever, launching themselves into outer space.
  • The Climax: The shuttle crash-lands on a strange, unknown planet. They are terrified until friendly green aliens approach them.
  • Resolution: The aliens peacefully repair the boys' shuttle, open the locked doors, and safely guide them back to Earth.

sections.title: 4. GLOSSARY & PICTIONARY
sections.content:
Vocabulary Word Definition / Meaning
DesolatedMade empty, uninhabitable, ruined, or bare.
HubA habitat; a central living space or settlement.
SolA Martian day (a day on planet Mars).
CapsuleA small cylinder-shaped bed or compartment for sleeping.
Space suitA specialized, pressurized suit worn by astronauts.
AstonishedGreatly surprised, amazed, or impressed.
Fossil FuelFuel formed from the remains of dead plants and animals over millions of years (e.g., coal, oil).
AdaptedModified or adjusted to survive in new conditions.
GalaxyA massive system consisting of millions or billions of stars.
Milky WayThe specific galaxy that contains our Solar System.

sections.title: 5. GRAMMAR (Let us Build & Let us Know)
sections.content:
A. Compound Words

A compound word is formed when two distinct words are combined to create a brand-new word with a completely different meaning.

  • ⭐ Sun + Flower = Sunflower
  • ⭐ Light + House = Lighthouse
  • ⭐ Mail + Box = Mailbox
  • ⭐ Watch + Man = Watchman
  • ⭐ Star + Fish = Starfish
  • ⭐ Rain + Bow = Rainbow
  • ⭐ Foot + Ball = Football

B. Nouns

1. Collective Noun: Names a group viewed as a single, whole unit.

  • 🐦 A flock of birds
  • 🐝 A swarm of bees
  • 🍇 A bunch of grapes
  • 🐜 An army of ants
  • 🐄 A herd of cattle
  • 🎵 A choir of singers

2. Abstract Noun: Names concepts, emotions, or qualities that cannot be physically touched.

  • 💭 Emotions: Happiness, sadness, anger, fear
  • 💡 Ideas: Freedom, justice, knowledge, truth
  • ✨ Qualities: Honesty, bravery, beauty, kindness

qa.q: What is the setting of the story 'Earth, the Desolated Home'?
qa.a: The story is set in the future, in the year 2068, on the planet Mars. Earth has been rendered uninhabitable due to human actions.

qa.q: How long did it take for the grandfather to reach Arivumathi?
qa.a: It took the grandfather 1500 sols (Martian days) to travel across Mars to reach Arivumathi's hub.

qa.q: How is water produced on Mars according to the lesson?
qa.a: Unlike Earth, where water is naturally available, water on Mars is artificially produced by burning fuels.

qa.q: Name the planets mentioned in the poem 'Beyond the Universe'.
qa.a: The poet mentions Earth, Mars, Saturn, Jupiter, Uranus, and Neptune during the space journey.

qa.q: How did Amuthan and Nilavan return to Earth?
qa.a: After crashing on an unknown planet, friendly green aliens approached them, repaired their damaged space shuttle, and safely guided them back home.

questions.question: What does the word 'Sol' represent in the story?
questions.explanation: According to the glossary in the lesson, a 'Sol' is the term used to describe a Martian day.

questions.question: Why do the vegetables grown on Mars lack real flavor in the year 2068?
options: They are grown without any water.
They are grown in the dark.
questions.explanation: The grandfather notes that unlike the natural, fertile soil on Earth, the soil on Mars is chemically treated, which causes the crops to lack real flavor.

questions.question: If you were a colonist living on Mars in 2068, how would you obtain water for your daily needs?
options: By pumping it from underground Martian rivers.
By artificially generating it through burning fuels.
By melting the ice giant Neptune.
questions.explanation: The lesson states that unlike on Earth where water is natural, water on Mars is artificially generated by burning fuels.

questions.question: Which of the following is the correct compound word formed by joining the words 'Star' and 'Fish'?
questions.explanation: A compound word combines two distinct words. 'Star' + 'Fish' creates the new word 'Starfish'.

questions.question: According to the poem 'Beyond the Universe', which planet is known as the 'blue ice giant'?
questions.explanation: The poet describes flying past Neptune, specifically referring to it as the blue ice giant.

questions.question: How did Amuthan and Nilavan manage to get back to Earth after their space shuttle crashed?
options: They fixed the shuttle themselves using a manual.
Friendly green aliens repaired their shuttle and guided them home.
Their grandfather tracked them down and towed them back.
They used an emergency teleportation device.
questions.explanation: After crashing on an unknown planet, friendly green aliens peacefully repaired their shuttle, opened the doors, and guided them safely back to Earth.

questions.question: What is the meaning of the word 'Hub' as used in the lesson?
options: A habitat or central living settlement
questions.explanation: In the glossary, a 'Hub' is defined as a habitat, a central living space, or settlement.

questions.question: Imagine you see a factory continuously dumping chemicals into a river, destroying the local ecosystem. Based on 'Earth, the Desolated Home', what is the ultimate risk of such human actions?
options: We will have to invent better water filters.
We will destroy our natural home and be forced to adapt to dead, harsh planets.
The river will eventually clean itself.
Aliens will come to repair our planet.
questions.explanation: The core message of the story is that human exploitation destroys our natural home (Earth), forcing us into harsh, artificial survival situations on dead planets like Mars.

questions.question: 'The choir sang a beautiful song.' What type of noun is the word 'choir'?
questions.explanation: A collective noun names a group of individuals viewed as a single unit. A 'choir' is a collective noun for a group of singers.

questions.question: Who are the two main characters in the supplementary story 'Lost in Space'?
questions.explanation: The story revolves around two friends named Amuthan and Nilavan who accidentally launch a space shuttle.

questions.question: What is the primary message conveyed in the prose 'Earth, the Desolated Home'?
options: Mars is a much better place to live than Earth.
Space travel is a dangerous but fun adventure.
We must protect Earth instead of destroying it and struggling to survive elsewhere.
India is the only country that can colonize Mars.
questions.explanation: The story compares the beautiful, natural past of Earth with the harsh reality of Mars, emphasizing the importance of protecting our natural home.

questions.question: In what year is the story 'Earth, the Desolated Home' set?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that the setting of the story is the year 2068.

questions.question: You are visiting an aerospace museum and see a warning sign on a shuttle control panel. Remembering what happened to Amuthan and Nilavan, what is the most likely consequence of pulling random levers?
options: You will win a prize from the museum.
The shuttle will safely take you on a guided tour.
You might trigger a dangerous, uncontrollable launch into an unknown environment.
The lever will simply not work.
questions.explanation: In 'Lost in Space', pulling a lever accidentally launched the boys into outer space, resulting in a terrifying crash landing.

questions.question: What does the word 'Astonished' mean?
questions.explanation: The glossary defines 'Astonished' as being greatly surprised, amazed, or impressed.

questions.question: Which of the following words represents an Abstract Noun?
questions.explanation: An abstract noun names a quality, idea, or emotion that cannot be physically touched. 'Bravery' is a quality.

questions.question: According to the poem 'Beyond the Universe', which planets does the poet pass before reaching Uranus?
questions.explanation: The poet's journey sequence goes from Earth to Mars, then Saturn, then Jupiter, before finally passing Uranus and Neptune.

questions.question: Where did Amuthan and Nilavan accidentally lock themselves?
options: Inside a functional space shuttle at a museum
questions.explanation: The story states that the boys were visiting a space museum and locked themselves inside a functional space shuttle.

questions.question: What is the definition of a 'Capsule' as mentioned in the text?
options: A chemical used to treat soil
A small cylinder-shaped bed or compartment for sleeping
questions.explanation: The glossary specifically defines a capsule as a small cylinder-shaped bed or compartment for sleeping.

questions.question: How long did it take for the grandfather to travel across Mars to visit Arivumathi?
questions.explanation: The text mentions that grandfather traveled 1500 sols (Martian days) to visit Arivumathi for her birthday.

questions.question: You are eating a meal grown in an artificial Martian colony in 2068. Based on the grandfather's experience, how will your food compare to food grown on Earth?
options: It will taste exactly the same.
It will lack real flavor because the soil is chemically treated.
It will be toxic and inedible.
questions.explanation: Grandfather explicitly notes that crops grown in the chemically treated soil on Mars lack the real, natural flavor of Earth's vegetables.

questions.question: What is the correct collective noun used for a group of bees?
questions.explanation: The grammar section lists 'a swarm of bees' as the correct collective noun.

questions.question: What is the name of the specific galaxy that contains our Solar System?
questions.explanation: The glossary defines the Milky Way as the specific galaxy that contains our Solar System.

questions.question: How many colonies did India establish on Mars by the year 2068?
questions.explanation: The text states that India has established three colonies on Mars.

questions.question: What is the main difference between an Abstract Noun and a Collective Noun?
options: Abstract nouns name places, while collective nouns name things.
Abstract nouns name physical objects, while collective nouns name emotions.
Abstract nouns name ideas/emotions you cannot physically touch, while collective nouns name a group of individuals viewed as one unit.
Abstract nouns are combined words, while collective nouns are single words.
questions.explanation: The grammar rules define collective nouns as naming groups (like a flock) and abstract nouns as naming concepts or feelings (like happiness or truth) that cannot be touched.

questions.question: In the poem 'Beyond the Universe', which planet is characterized by its red storms?
questions.explanation: The poem describes Jupiter as the solar system's giant, characterized by its red storms.

questions.question: Your friend describes the 'freedom' and 'joy' of running in an open field. What kind of nouns are the words 'freedom' and 'joy'?
questions.explanation: 'Freedom' (an idea) and 'joy' (an emotion) are things that cannot be physically touched or seen, making them abstract nouns.

questions.question: Who repaired the damaged space shuttle for the boys in 'Lost in Space'?
questions.explanation: After the crash, friendly green aliens approached the boys, repaired the shuttle, and helped them get home.

questions.question: What does the word 'Adapted' mean in the context of surviving on Mars?
options: Modified or adjusted to survive in new conditions
questions.explanation: The glossary defines 'Adapted' as being modified or adjusted to survive in new, different conditions.

questions.question: What kind of fuel is formed from the remains of dead plants and animals over millions of years?
questions.explanation: The glossary defines 'Fossil Fuel' (like coal or oil) as fuel formed from the remains of dead plants and animals.

questions.question: Which of the following is the correct compound word formed by combining 'Mail' and 'Box'?
questions.explanation: The grammar section shows that combining 'Mail' + 'Box' creates the compound word 'Mailbox'.

questions.question: In the year 2068, where have humans colonized and created new habitats?
questions.explanation: Earth has been destroyed, and humans have established new colonies on Mars, known as the Red Planet.

questions.question: Why does a person require a space suit to walk outside on Mars?
options: Because the gravity is too strong.
Because the environment is toxic and lacks natural, breathable air.
Because there are dangerous aliens outside.
Because it is a fashion requirement in 2068.
questions.explanation: The text contrasts Earth's clean, free air with Mars, where the environment is harsh and toxic, requiring a space suit and an oxygen cylinder.

questions.question: Where is the final destination of the poet's journey in 'Beyond the Universe'?
options: Beyond the Milky Way galaxy into the infinite universe to meet the Creator
questions.explanation: The poem concludes with the traveler flying beyond the Milky Way into the infinite universe to meet the Creator.

questions.question: How does the poem describe the planet Saturn?
options: As a planet with magnificent rings and moons.
As a giant with red storms.
questions.explanation: In the cosmic journey described in the poem, Saturn is marveled at for its magnificent rings and moons.

questions.question: What color is the planet Mars commonly referred to in the text?
questions.explanation: Mars is explicitly referred to as 'the Red Planet' in both the prose and the poem.

questions.question: How does agriculture on Earth in the past contrast with agriculture on Mars in 2068?
options: Earth had chemically treated soil, while Mars has natural soil.
Both planets rely entirely on fossil fuels to grow crops.
Earth had natural, fertile soil yielding tasty vegetables, while Mars uses chemically treated soil yielding flavorless crops.
Plants on Mars grow much larger and tastier than on Earth.
questions.explanation: The grandfather compares the tasty vegetables grown in Earth's natural soil to the flavorless crops grown in the chemically treated soil on Mars.

questions.question: What is the meaning of the word 'Desolated'?
options: Made empty, uninhabitable, ruined, or bare
questions.explanation: According to the glossary, 'Desolated' means made empty, uninhabitable, ruined, or bare.

questions.question: Whom did the grandfather travel across Mars to visit?
questions.explanation: The plot of 'Earth, the Desolated Home' states that the grandfather travels 1500 sols to visit Arivumathi for her birthday.

questions.question: What did the friendly aliens who helped the boys look like?
questions.explanation: The supplementary story describes the aliens that approached the boys as 'friendly green aliens.'

questions.question: Why did Amuthan and Nilavan go into outer space?
options: They were sent on a mission by India.
They accidentally pulled a lever inside a shuttle at a museum.
They were abducted by green aliens.
questions.explanation: The story 'Lost in Space' explains that the launch was an accident caused by them pulling a lever while locked inside a functional shuttle at a museum.
